_note: ORIGINAL content. Topic coverage was checked against javascript.info and Eloquent JavaScript. No prose, examples, or exercises were copied; the references are curriculum guides only.
title: DOM & Events
summary: Connect JavaScript behavior to page elements and user actions.
refresher: The DOM represents the page as objects. Select an element, verify it exists, then register an event listener. The listener runs later when the browser reports an action such as a click or input change.

blocks.body: JavaScript in a browser becomes interactive by working with the Document Object Model. An element object exposes properties such as `textContent` and methods such as `addEventListener`, letting code respond without reloading the whole page.

`querySelector` returns the first matching element or null. An event listener receives an event object that describes what happened. The listener should update the smallest necessary part of the interface and keep data decisions separate from display changes.

blocks.caption: The setup registers one listener. Each click then updates the stored count and the page text.
blocks.output: The displayed number increases after each click.

blocks.caption: Selection can fail when the id is wrong or the script runs before the element exists. Check the returned value before using it.

blocks.body: The setup code runs once and registers the callback. It does not run the callback immediately. Each later click makes the browser call the listener, which changes count and then writes the new value into the output element.

blocks.title: Use textContent for plain text
blocks.body: `innerHTML` asks the browser to parse markup. Use `textContent` for ordinary user-facing text, especially when a value came from input, so text is displayed rather than treated as page structure.

blocks.prompt: What can `querySelector` return when no element matches?
blocks.why: The method returns null when it cannot find a matching element, so code should check before accessing properties.

blocks.prompt: When does a click listener run?
choices: After the registered element receives a click
As soon as it is registered
Before the page loads every time
Only when the function returns text
blocks.why: Registration stores the callback for later; the browser invokes it when the matching click event occurs.

blocks.title: DOM & Events checklist
items: Check a selector result before use.
